inscription.head:
No. 277.
(A.R. No. 431 of 1913.)
ĀṞAGAḶŪR, ATTUR TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Kāmēśvara temple, central shrine, on the north base of the maṇḍapa in
front.

p.b:
Vira-Kampaṇṇa-uḍaiyar Śaka 1297, Rākshasa, Vṛiśchika,
śu. 11, Friday, Rōhiṇī. Irregular.

inscription.p:
It is stated that the controlling administrative authorities, śrī-rudrar, śrī-
māhēśvarar, dēvakaṉmi and accountant of the temple (kōyiṟ-kaṇakkar) in the
temple of Tirukkāmīśvaram-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār, the God (uḍaiyār) of Āṟagaḻūr in
Āṟṟūr-kkūṟṟam in Malāḍu alias Jaṉanātha-vaḷanāḍu assembled in the Āṟai-
nāyakaṉ-tirumaṇḍapam and granted the palamudal-kaṇkāṇippaṇi out of the
several kaṇkāṇi-ppaṇis in the temple and a house-site along with appertaining
privileges as kāṇi to Eṉṉuḍaiya-nāyaṉār, son of Māṟātena-uḷḷattiruindār alias
Aghōra-Śiva-mudaliyār. This kāṇi was to be enjoyed by the grantee along with
his brothers for all time.

l.ta:
tipati śrīariyaṇṇa uṭaiyar kumāraṉ

inscription.head:
No. 278.
(A.R. No. 432 of 1913.)
ĀṞAGAḶŪR, ATTUR TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Kāmēśvara temple, central shrine, on the north base of the maṇḍapa
infront.

p.b: Jaṭāvarmaṉ Tribhuvanachakravartti Sundarapāṇḍya Year 13, Mithuna, śu. 13, Friday. Anusham^1^

inscription.p:
It is stated that sometime before, there was a disagreement between the
vāṇiga-nagarattār and cheṭṭis, whereby the former humiliated the latter, in respect
of the conduct of the festivals in the months of Āṉi, Mārgaḻi and Māśi for
God Tirukkāmīśvaram-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār, the God (uḍaiyār) of Āṟagaḻūr in Āṟṟūr-
kūṟṟam in Malāḍu alias Jaṉaṉātha-vaḷanāḍu. Apparently as a consequence of the
above dispute the temple sold it as chaṇḍēśvara-pperuvilai to the former body
the privilege of providing the various items required (listed) for conducting the
stated festivals which had suffered. However, in due course, the festivals suffered
(due to the nonreceipt of the required items from those who had taken over
the responsibility to supply them), seeing this, the nāṭṭavar of Āṟṟūr intervened
and granted (the village ?) Talaivāy in Āṟṟūr-nāḍu as iṟai-ili (tax-free grant) with
exemption from all āyams (levies) including poṉ-vari as also nāṭṭu-viṉiyōgam
(contributions towards the expenses of the nāḍu) to enable the conduct of the
stated festivals without any short-coming or interruptions. However it was
stipulated that the kaḍamai which was outside the jurisdiction (kāṇi-ppaṟṟu),
Āṟṟūr-nāḍu might be collected (from the granted village).

inscription.head:
No. 279.
(A.R. No. 433 of 1913.)
ĀṞAGAḶŪR, ATTUR TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Kāmēśvara temple, central shrine, on the north base of the maṇḍapa
infront.

p.b:
Achyutadēva-mahārāya Śaka 1461, Vikāri, Kanyā, śu. 7,
Friday, Mūlam = 1539 A.D.,
September 19.

inscription.p:
The record is erased and damaged. It records the grant of māṭhāpatya-kkāṇi
(the privilege of controlling the maṭha attached to the temple ?) and the
administration of the temple (kōvil ūḻiyam) along with the rights and privileges
attached probably to Āṟṟūr-nāṭṭu-vēḷār, by the several authorities of the temple
acting on behalf of the God Tirukkāmīśvaram-uḍaiya-tambīrāṉār of Āṟagaḷūr
in Āṟṟūr-kūṟṟam in Magadai-maṇḍalam in Malāḍu alias Jaṉanātha-vaḷanāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 280.
(A.R. No. 434 of 1913.)
ĀṞAGAḶŪR, ATTUR TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Kāmēśvara temple, central shrine, on the north base of the maṇḍapa
infront.

Records a minor order (chiṟu-muṟi) granting the temples of Tirukkāmīśvaram-
uḍaiya-nāyaṉār, Tāyilu-nalla-chōḷiśvaram-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār, Poṉamparappiṉa-chōḷiśvaram-
uḍaiya-nāyaṉār, as kāṇi to Chēramāṉ. The grantee was further given as kāṇi, 100
kuḻi of three crop wet-land each in the central portion (naḍuppaṟṟu) of
Kuṟukkaipāḍi and Pākkaṉpāḍi as tax-free (iṟai-ili) land and 1000 kuḻi of varaku-
growing dry-crop land each in Ilattipāḍi and Viśalūr. The grantee could enjoy
the latter kāṇi as long as the sun and moon last. Kulaśēkhara-brahmarāyar and
Chētarāya-brahmarāyar figure among signatories.

inscription.head:
No. 281.
(A.R. No. 435 of 1913.)
ĀṞAGAḶŪR, ATTUR TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Kāmēśvara temple, central shrine, on the north base of the maṇḍapa
in front.

This is a record of a political compact through which Cheṅgkēṇi Ammai
Appaṉ Aḻagiya-chōḻa-chchambuvarāyaṉ had sought to ensure that Aiyyar
Poṉparappiṉa-vāṇakōvaraiyar, Aiyyar Kulōttuṅga-chōḻa-vāṇakōvaraiyar, and
brother-in-law (maichchunanār) Kāḍavarāyar looked upto him without doing any
wrong to him and himself undertook to reciprocate and do no wrong to them.
If ever he failed in honouring this compact and did harm to them, he could
be deemed to be the bearer of the sandals and betel box of his enemies and
not as the son of his father. He swore on this taking the name of Pallavaraiyaṉ.
The compact document was to be inscribed on the walls of the temple of
Tirukkāmīśvaram-uḍaiyār of Āṟagaḷūr.

svastiśrī [|*] tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīkulottuṅka
coḻatevaṟku yāṇṭu 35vatu aiyya[r]

inscription.head:
No. 282.
(A.R. No. 436 of 1913.)
ĀṞAGAḶŪR, ATTUR TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Kāmēśvara temple, central shrine, behind the dvārapāla image in the
maṇḍapa in front, right of entrance.
The right hand and bottom portions of this inscription are built in.

It records a gift of 2000 aṉṟāḍu-naṟ-kāśu for one perpetual lamp to be
burnt before the God Tirukkāmīśvaram-uḍaiyār, by a brahmādhirājar. Also
refers to the endowment of 49 cows, at seven each for seven sandhi lamps.

l.note: pari is written above the lines.

rājar pakkal ikkoyil muppatu vaṭṭattu

inscription.head:
No. 283.
(A.R. No. 437 of 1913.)
ĀṞAGAḶŪR, ATTUR TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Kāmēśvara temple, central shrine, On the wall behind the dvārapāla image
in the maṇḍapa in front, left of entrance.
The record is built in all around.

This inscription records the endowment by Nāchchi, sister of [Toṇ]ḍaimāṉ,
for the food offerings to Periya-pē[ṟu]-mudaliyār in the temple of Tirukkā[mīśvaram-
uḍaiyār] at Āṟagaḷūr.

l.note: It must be referred to RAjarāja I on palaeographical grounds.

l.ta: kāṇiyāka kaikkoṇṭu nāḷ oṉṟukku iru ṉāḻi

inscription.head:
No. 284.
(A.R. No. 438 of 1913.)
ĀṞAGAḶŪR, ATTUR TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Kāmēśvara temple, second prākāra, south wall.

This inscription records a (royal) order addressed to the tānattār of the
temple of Tirukkāmīśvaramuḍaiya-nāyaṉār, the God of Āṟagaḷūr in Āṟṟūr-
kūṟṟam, directing that the weavers settled by them outside the northern gate
of Āṟagaḷūr may be assigned as the cultivators (kuḍi-makkaḷ) of the dēvadāna
(lands) of God Kulaśēkhara-āḷuḍaiyār consecreted by Ka[ṇ]ḍiya-dēvar in their
temple with effect from the date cited. The order was attested by Ādi[tta]ṉ
Gaṇapatiyāḻvāṉ alias Kāḍuveṭṭi of Kappalūr alias Ulagaḷanta-chōḻanallūr of
(cf. 275).

uṭaiyār tirukāmiśvaramuṭaiya nāyaṉār koyilt tāṉattārk[ku]

inscription.head:
No. 285.
(A.R. No. 439 of 1913.)
ĀṞAGAḶŪR, ATTUR TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Kāmēśvara temple, second prākāra, south wall.

p.b:
Tribhuvanachakravartti Sundara-pāṇḍya Year 1[8], Mēsham śu. 15
(Pūrṇṇami), Thursday, Svāti
-1289 A.D., April 7 (?)
p.note:
The year has been read doubtfully as 12 in the Annual Report. It looks more like 18. On
this basis assuming that the Sundara-pāṇḍya of this record is identical with JaTAvarman
Sundara-paṇḍya whose accession we calculated to have been 1271 A.D., The equivalant has
been given as above. (Swamikannu Pillai, L.D., on some new dates of Pāṇḍya kings in the
13th century A.D., Ind. Ant., Vol. XLII 1913), p. 221-223). It is seen that the king could also have
been the JaTAvarman Sundara-pāṇḍya II whose initial year is stated to be 1302 A.D., as the
above details of date also equate with 1319 A.D., April 5, f.d.n. ·29 (cf A.R. Ep., 1913-14 p. 65).

inscription.p: The beginning of the first twelve lines of this inscription has been lost.

It records the sale of the rights of worship on 3-1/8 days (in each month)
along with appertaining rights and privileges to Tiruñānasambanda-paṇḍitar, for
50 paṇam, equal to 25,000 chōḻiya-kāśu by the śivabrāhmaṇas of the temple of
[Tirukkāmīśvaram]-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār. These 3-(1/8) days worship had fallen to the lot
of the śivabrāhmaṇas consequent on the establishment of the guilt of stealing
an ornament belonging to the temple, by a lady, who and whose relatives held
the right of worship for these 3-(1/8) days and which were ordered by the temple-
authorities to be confiscated and assigned to the rest of the right holders who
were asked to pay the price of 250 paṇam for the same. The latter could not
find the required money and that is why the rights for these many days were
now sold as stated above as the amount realised had to be paid to the temple.

inscription.head:
No. 286.
(A.R. No. 440 of 1913.)
ĀṞAGAḶŪR, ATTUR TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Kāmēśvara temple, second prākāra, east wall, right of entrance.

Records a political compact between Āṟagaḷūruḍaiya Irājarāja-dēvaṉ
Poṉparappinār alias Kulōttuṅga-chōḻa-Vāṇakōvaraiyar and Kiḷiyūr Maḷaiyamāṉ
••••• of Tirukkōvālūr, each of them undertaking to come to the aid of the
other in times of distress. River Āḻvīṉai-āṟu was to be the boundary of the
territories under their control.

inscription.head:
No. 287.
(A.R. No. 441 of 1913.)
ĀṞAGAḶŪR, ATTUR TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Kāmēśvara temple, second prākāra, east wall, left of entrance.

Records a royal order addressed to the tānattār of the temple of
Tirukkāmīśvaramuḍaiya-nāyaṉar of Āṟagaḷūr in Āṟṟūr-kūṟṟam granting 3000 kuḻi
of wet land that would be reclaimed after clearing, as tax-free (iṟai-ili) and
including poṉvari and all such levies (āyam-income) as well as āḷ-amaiñji-tēvai
(free labour or payment in lieu as was required to be tendered for the functioning
of the village management) to serve as capital for the carrying out of the worship
and for (periodical) maintenance of the deities Kulaśēkhara-āḷuḍaiyār and
Vāḻvittamaṅgai (in shrines set up) in the temple of Tirukkāmīśvaram-uḍaiya-
nāyaṉar at Āṟagaḷūr in Āṟṟūr-kūṟṟam. The record was attested by •••• ṉ Gaṇapati-
āḻvāṉ alias Kāḍuveṭṭi of Kappalūr alias Ulagāḷandachōḷa-nallūr in Muttūr-
kkūṟṟam in Pāṇḍi-maṇḍalam. The grant was to take effect from the dated cited
above.

inscription.head:
No. 288.
(A.R. No. 442 of 1913.)
ĀṞAGAḶŪR, ATTUR TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Kāmēśvara temple, second Prākāra, east wall, left of entrance.

Records a royal order addressed to the tāṉattār of the temple of
Tirukkāmīśvaram-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār granting one vēli of first grade wet land out
of the vāra-paṟṟu of Āṟṟūr, as tiru-nandavana-ppuṟa-iṟai-ili (tax free land granted
for the sake of the garden attached to the temple) for the maintenance (jīvaṉam)
of those tending the temple garden. The poṉvari and all such āyams (levies) as
also āḷ-amaiñji-tēvai (free-labour or payment in lieu) as assessed on the land
granted could also be collected and utilised for the same purpose. The record
was attested by Ādittaṉ Gaṇapati-āḻvāṉ alias Kāḍuveṭṭi of Kappalūr alias
Ulagaḷanda-chōḻanallūr in Muttūr-kūṟṟam in Pāṇḍi-maṇḍalam.

inscription.head:
No. 289.
(A.R. No. 443 of 1913.)
inscription.p: ĀṞAGAḶŪR, ATTUR TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
Kāmēśvara temple, second prākāra, west wall, left of entrance.

Records a royal order addressed to the tāṉattār of the temple of
Tirukkāmīśvaram-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār of Āragaḷūr, granting 3500 kuḻi measured by
vaṉṉenjaṉ-kōl of first grade wet land irrigated by the big reservoir (periya-ēri)
in Alampaḷam in Naraiyūr-kūṟṟam in Milāḍu alias Jaṉaṉātha-vaḷanāḍu, and
[1]5,000 kuḻi of dry-crop-land, out of land held by the king (nam-paṟṟu), as
tirunāmattukkāṇi (land directly held by the God/temple), to serve as capital to
provide the paddy and cash required for providing food-offerings and decoration
to the God during the service Ulagu-muḻuduḍaiyār-sandhi instituted in the name
of the king (for the God of their temple). The temple-authorities (tāṉattār) could
enlist kuḍis (for working the land) as they pleased and could raise any crop
according to their wish during the cultivation seasons, kār (rain-fed) and paśāṉam.
The king agreed to forego the levies kaḍamai, poṉ-vari, kāśāyam, kāttikai-
ppachchai,mātiri sānti nall-erudu, naṟ-paśu, as due from that land. The right to
one half of the maintenance granted to the one appointed to write the accounts
of the ūr, constituting the āḷ-amañji-tēvai, echōṟu and such in respect of that
land could also be collected and utilised. The rights, nā-ūrumai and kālurimai in
respect of that land were also authorised to be enjoyed. The record was attested
by [Chiṟinaṅgū]ruḍaiyāṉ.

One noteworthy feature in the record is that recording of the extent of
dry-crop-land has been tampered with. What was originally 5000 kuḻi has been
altered to 15,000 by correcting the word kuḻi to padi and the total extent of
land was also made into 18500 kuḻi.

[svasti]śrī [|*] aruḷicceyal āṟakaḷur uṭaiyār

inscription.head:
No. 290.
(A.R. No. 444 of 1913.)
ĀṞAGAḶŪR, ATTUR TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Kāmēśvara temple, second prākāra, west wall, left of entrance.

Records a royal order addressed to the tānattār of the temple of
Tirukkāmīśva[ra]-muḍaiya-nāyaṉār of Āṟagaḷūr in Āṟṟūr-kkūṟṟam intimating the
grant of 4 vēli of wet land of first grade in the vāra-ppaṟṟu of Āṟṟūr as tax-
free (iṟai-ili) including the (entitlement to ?) poṉ-vari, kāśāyam, kāttigai-pachchai
and such levies (āyam) as also āḷ-amañji-tēvai for feeding ten āṇḍārs (mendicants)
in the temple. The land concerned was ordered to be deleted from puravu and
vari (registers containing the details of the royal dues from the land under
cultivation). The record was attested by Āditaṉ Gaṇapati-āḻvāṉ alias Kāḍuveṭṭi
of Kappalūr alias Ulagaḷanda-chōḻa-nallūr in Muttūr-kūṟṟam in Pāṇḍi-maṇḍalam.

inscription.head:
No. 291.
(A.R. No. 445 of 1913.)
ĀṞAGAḶŪR, ATTUR TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Karivaradarāja-Perumāḷ temple, central shrine, north base.

p.b:
Dhanma-rāyar, son of
Śāḷuva-Narasiṅga-r[āy]ar Śaka 1444, Duṉmati, Siṁha śu.
10 [Monday], Hastam. Irregular.

inscription.p:
The date quoted is wrong in every respect. The business portion of the record
is also very clumsily drafted. The inscription records that the tāṉattār of the
temple of Karivaṟ, appropriated the aḍimai-kāṇi (apportionment for engaging
slaves ?) given by the kaikkōḷar (weavers ?) and gave away one female as the
aḍiyāḷ of the God in view of the campaign of conquest being undertaken by
Narasā-nāyakkar. The remuneration (paḍittaram) to her from the said temple
was one nāḻi of cooked rice, (daily) and a house-site in the temple-precints
(maḍai-viḷākam), apparently to facilitate these payments to her the donor allotted
an tiru-viḍai-āṭṭam-charu (sarva-mānyam) 60 kuḻi of wet land irrigated by
Deviyar-kuṟuchchi-ēri and 300 kuḻi of dry-crop land in Gaṇḍamāttaṉ compared
No 450 below.

l.note: śrī is written close to svasti and man is written after a gap.

orumaṉaiyum ti[ru]vi[ṭ]ai āṭṭam caṟuvamāṉiyam āṉa
teviyarkuṟucci eri kiḻ nañcai nilattile aṟupatu kuḻiyum
tiruviṭai āṭṭam caṟumāṉa iṟai[ili] kaṇṭa māttān puñcai
nilattil irunūṟu kuḻiyum oru taṟi māṉiyamum kuṭuttapaṭiyāle

itukku ahitam paṇṇinavaṉ

inscription.head:
No. 292.
(A.R. No. 446 of 1913.)
ĀṞAGAḶŪR, ATTUR TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Karivaradarāja-Perumāḷ temple, central shrine, north base.

Records an order of Vāṇakōvaraiyaṉ addressed to tiruvaḍi-ppiḍippār, śrī-
bhaṇḍārigaḷ and śrī-vaishṇava-kkaṇkāṇi-cheyvār of the temple of Kariya-perumāḷ
of Āragaḷūr with regard to the tirunāmatti-kkāṇi of 3170(3/4) kuḻi of wet land and
7115 kuḻi of karai-ppuñchēy land (dry crop land on the outskirts ?) and 14 Iluppai
trees and one mango tree, in the kāṇippaṟṟu (land that can be allotted for services
to servants ?) adjacent to the channel in Kāśāṟaṉpaṭṭu alias Jayadhara
chcharuppēdimaṅgalam, a tax-free tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam of the above said God, allotted
in several individuals who however were not managing the lands and making
the due contributions to the temple. Now, those lands were disposed as vīra-
māgadha-pperuvilai and 33, 254 kāśu was collected at the rate of 6 kāśu (per
kuḻi) of wet land and 2 kāśu per kuḻi of karai-ppuñchey land. These proceeds
were taken into his treasury (charakku) by the chief ?)

inscription.head:
No. 293.
(A.R. No. 447 of 1913.)
ĀṞAGAḶŪR, ATTUR TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Karivaradarāja-perumāḷ temple, central shrine, north wall.
In late characters.

This inscription records that Puṇṇiyavāṭṭiyār, the wife of Irājarājaṉ-
Vāṇakōvaraiyaṉ and daughter of Kuḻūruḍaiyāṉ Iraṇaṉ Kōvalarāyaṉ, carried out
repairs in the śrī-Vimānam (central shrine) and temple of Irājarāja-viṇṇagar (i.e.,)
temple of Śrī-Perumāḷ.

edition.l:

inscription.head:
No. 294.
(A.R. No. 448 of 1913.)
ĀṞAGAḶŪR, ATTUR TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Karivaradarāja-perumāḷ temple, central shrine, east base.

p.note:
As per L.D. Swamikannu Pillai (A.R. Ep. 1914 p. 65), the eclipse referred to could be the
lunar one that occurred on September 9, 1280 A.D. f.d.t. ·38, the date conciding with the
13th year of Māṟavarman Kulaśēkhara (I). However as a lunar eclipse occurs only in the
concluding phase of the full moon and when the eclipse ends in the first phase of the dark
fortnight (prarthamā) and as per his own Indian Ephemeries) on September 10 it is very
doubtful that the eclipse on September 10 was visible in these regions. However, as per his
own Indian Ephemeris it would appear their only the concluding phase of the a lunar eclipse
also occurred in the month of PuraṭṭAdi both in 1279 A.D. (September 21-full moon ·75)
and in 1281 A.D. (August 30-fullmoon lasted till ·04 of August 31).

inscription.p:
This inscription records a royal order addressed to tiru-vaḍi-ppiḍikkum-
nambimār and śrī-vaishṇava-kkaṇkāṇi-chayvār of the temple of Kariya-perumāḷ
in Āṟagaḷūr in Āṟṟūr-kkūṟṟam, intimating them that he had granted as
tirunāmattukkāṇi-iṟai-ili (land granted tax-free, to be held directly by the God
along with the entitlement to collect and enjoy the proceeds of the levies (āyam)
poṉ-vari, kāśāyam, kāttikai-ppachchai, uśi-vāśi, mātru-śaṉti, nall-erudu, naṟ-paśu,
vēli-erudu-kāṇikkaī, nāṭṭu-viṉiyōgam, āḷ-amañji-tēvai, 1000 kūḻi of first grade wet-
land in the vāra-ppaṟṟu under the reservoir in Poṉparappi alias Irāja-nārāyaṇa-
nallūr for the food-offerings (amudu-paḍi) and decoration (chāttu-paḍi) of (the
deity) Siṅgar in their temple, as propitiation in view of the eclipse in the month
of Puraṭṭādi in the year 13 of the kings elder brother (aṇāḻvi) Sundara-pāṇḍiya-
dēvar (the text as it is, suggests that the year quoted was in the regnal reckoning
of the elder brother rather than of the issuer of the record. It does not specify
that the benefaction was for the merit of aṇṇāḻvi). The record was attested by
Ādittaṉ Gaṇapati-āḻvāṉ alias Vāṇādarājan of Kappalūr alias Ulagaḷanda-
chōḻanallūr in Muttu-kkūṟṟam in Pāṇḍi-maṇḍalam.

inscription.head:
No. 295.
(A.R. No. 449 of 1913.)
ĀṞAGAḶŪR, ATTUR TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Karivaradarāja-perumāḷ temple, central shrine, east base.

p.b:
Kṛishṇadēva-mahārāja Śaka 1441, Pramādi, Mithuna,
śu. 13, Friday, Anurādhā =
1519 A.D., June 10.

inscription.p:
This record states that the local royal representive (rāśakaram) was causing
harm to Dēviyar-kuṟichchi village under the enjoyment (as tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam) of
God Kariyavar, the Perumāḷ of Āṟagaḻūr in Āṟṟūr-kūṟṟam in Magadai-maṇḍalam
in Malāḍu alias Jananātha-vaḷanāḍu and consequently the authorities of the temple
(sthānikar) went in deputation to Vijayanagar, the capital. Amaram Timmaraśar
secured an audience with the emperor for them and got Deviyar-kuṟuchchi
confirmed as free from all imports (sarvva-mānyam). Apparently the deputation
consisted of three temple-priests (brahmins) who were brothers and some of
Veṉṟu-mālaiyiṭṭa-perumāḷ of Ātrēya-gōtram and Vaikhānasa-sūtram and they
were conferred the right of worship in the temple (archana-vibhava-kkāṇi) (of
God Kariyavar). Each was honoured with green rope, a cap, a horse and umbrella.
Further as remunaration for their management of the temple, they were granted
perpetually 300 kuḻi of land out of the 1000 kuḻi of wet land under the reservoir
Poṉparappi in Tillai-kuṟuchchipaṟṟu and 600 kuḻi of wet land under the reservoir
of Dēviyar-kuṟichchi, both of which blocs of land were part of the land attached
to the temple (tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam of the God stated above). The land was granted
to them free of taxes (iṟai-ili) for the specific purpose of management of the
temple (sthāna-mānyaṁ) and was also freed from all tax obligations (sarvva-
mānyaṁ) (of. No. 450 below).

l.gr:
n pūrvva dakṣiṇa
paścima uttara catuca

inscription.head:
No. 296.
(A.R. No. 450 of 1913.)
ĀṞAGAḶŪR, ATTUR TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Karivaradarāja-perumāḷ temple, central shrine, east base.

p.note: Actually it is written as Kammajiya-rāyan.
p.b:
son of Śāḷuva Narasin4ga-rāyar Śaka 1444, Rudhirōdgāri,
Siṁha, śu. 10, Friday, Svāti.
Irregular.
p.note:
cf. No. 445 above. In both the Śaka year (given as No. 1444 but the cyclic year are different.
In both the months and tithi are siṁha 10 but the work day and asterism are different.

inscription.p:
This record is to be compared with No. 291 above. In both, the benefaction
was a view of the campign of conquest being undertaken by Narasā-nāyakkar.
Ammaṇa-nāyakkar who figures in No. 291 as the donor is stated here to have
been the son of Narasā-nāyakkar and as the one for whose sake the benefaction
was made by Tammaiya nāyakkar Ammaṇa-nāyakkar and also figures as the
signatory in the present record.

In the context as stated above, the inscription records the assignment of 450
(kuḻi) of wet land under the irrigation system of the reservoir of Chiṟṟēri, a
hamlet of Āragaḷūr, a portion of Gaṇḍamāttān dry crop land and 100 (kuḻi) of
wet land in •••• ka-rōyan-ēndal, all out of the land belonging to the temple,
freed from taxes and all other encumbarances as sarva-mānya again to three
brahmin priests who held the right (kaṇi) of worship-(archanā-vibhava-kāṇi)
in the temple of God Kariyvar in Āṟagaḷūr in Āṟṟūr-kkūṟṟam in Magada-
maṇḍalam in Malāḍu alias Jananātha-vaḷanāḍu for hereditary enjoyment.

svasti [|*] śrīman mahāmaṇḍaliśvaran hariyarāyara vipāṭan

inscription.head:
No. 297.
(A.R. No. 451 of 1913.)
ĀṞAGAḶŪR, ATTUR TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Karivaradarāja-perumāḷ temple, central shrine, south base of the
maṇḍapa in front.

p.b:
Kṛishṇadēva-mahārāyar Śaka 1446, Tāruṇa, Makara,
Ēkādaśi, Rēvati, Friday,
saṅkramaṇa. Irregular.

inscription.p:
The record gives the descent of the king from Tammarāya through Vīra-
pratāpa Narasā-nāyakkar and Vīra-bhōga-Vasantarāyar, though the relationship
inter se is not mentioned.

It registers the allotment of eight persons as Emberumāṉ-aḍiyār (servants
of the God-priests ?) for doing service to God Kariya-perumāḷ of Āṟagaḻur in
Āṟṟūr-kūṟṟam in Magadha-maṇḍalam in Malāḍu alias Jananātha-vaḷanāḍu by
Mṟuttañjeya-nāyakkar, the agent of Tirumalai-nāyakkar for the merit of his
master and grant of 1000 kuḻi of wet land fed by Rāghavēśvaran-channel in
Naraiyūr-kūṟṟam in the chīrmai on the southern bank, out of the bloc (chīrmai)
given to him by Tirumalai-nāyakkar, free from all encumberances (saṟva-
mānyam) and free from taxes (to those eight aḍiyār) for their own maintenance
and for providing food-offerings (amudu-paḍi) to the God. While referring to
the exemptions granted the following levies are mentioned-aṛai-vari (tax for
the maintenance of the dam), ñīr-vaḷi-kāṇikkai, nāṭṭu-vechcham-vilaṭam, paḻa-vari,
and pudu-vari. The grant was made at the auspicious time of shaṅkiramam
(saṅkramaṇam).

l.gr:
svasti [|*] śrīman mahāmaṇḍalēśvaran rācādirācan
rājaparamēśva[ra*]n śrīman pū[r]vva dakṣiṇa paścima

inscription.head:
No. 298.
(A.R. No. 452 of 1913.)
ĀṞAGAḶŪR, ATTUR TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Karivaradarāja-perumāḷ temple, central shrine, north base of the
maṇḍapa in front.

p.b:
Achyuta-dēva-mahārāya Śaka 1454, Nandana, Kuṁbha,
śu. 3, Monday, Uttiraṭṭādi.
Irregular.
p.note:
Probably the equivalent is 1533 A.D., January 27, Monday. On that day, it was śu. 2 upto
·42 and PUraṭṭAdi commenced after ·03.

inscription.p:
This inscription records the resolution of śēnai-mudali-āḻvār, and the śrī-
tāṉattār, having authority on account of their management of the temple, of
the temple of Kariya-perumāḷ, having met in the sōpāna-maṇḍapam infront of
the (shrine of) the God, to grant kāṇi-āṭchi consisting of a house site, a loom,
50 kuḻi of land out of the tiru-viḍai-āṭṭam land of the God in Poṉparappi and
one nāḷi of the food offered to God daily, to Kaṇṇaṉ and his brother and to
the former's progeny, in view of the former having instituted a car festival (?).
Kaṇṇaṉ was a kaikkōḷaṉ. This kāṇi-āṭchi was capable of being sold and mortgaged.

l.gr:
śubhamastu [|*] svasti [|*] śrīmanmahāmaṇḍalēśvaran hariya
rāyara vibhāṭan bhāṣai
l.note: ṇṭa ṣeems to be written one below the other.
lu ṣeems to be written below the line.
mo written one below the line.

inscription.head:
No. 299.
(A.R. No. 453 of 1913.)
ĀṞAGAḶŪR, ATTUR TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Karivaradarāja-perumāḷ temple, Kamalamaṅgai shrine, north, wall.
The beginning portion of the line of this inscription is erased and damaged.

It records a royal order addressed to the tānattār of the temple of
Vayirāvaṇa-Īśvaram uḍaiya-nāyaṉār intimating the grant of 1000 kuḻi out of the
vāra-ppaṟṟu lands under the irrigation of the big reservoir of Toḻuvūr in Magada-
maṇḍalam, as dēvadāna-iṟai-ili (tax-free on account of the land being granted
to the temple) including the cash levies (kāśāyam) and poṉvari and all other
levies (āyam) for the worship and repairs to the temple of a God (name erased).

inscription.head:
No. 300.
(A.R. No. 454 of 1913.)
ĀṞAGAḶŪR, ATTUR TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Karivaradarāja-perumāḷ temple, Kamalamaṅgai shrine, north wall.

It records a royal order addressed for execution to Vilāḍarāyar and
puravariyār (officials in charge of taxes) intimating that the village Kuṟukkaipāḍi
viz, levies, poṉvari, kāśyam, kāttikai-ppachchai nall-erudu, naṟ-paśu, ūśivāśi, mātru-
śānti and other kinds of levies, taṟiy-iṟai, maniy-iṟai,chekk-iṟai, āṭṭ-iṟai-ppāṭṭam
and magamai had been assigned as dēvadāṉa-iṟaili, for the worship and
maintenance of the premises of God Tāyilum-nalla-chōḷīśvaram-uḍaiya-nāyanār.
The record was attested by Vāṇakōvaraiyaṉ and Viḻuppādarāyaṉ.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [|*] vilāṭarāyarum puravariyārum ceyakkaṭavapaṭi
kuṟukkaipāṭi nāṉkellaikkuṭ ••••• [ṉ]cey puṉcey
karaip puṉcey nattap puṉcey ental paṟṟu

coḷi

inscription.head:
No. 301.
(A.R. No. 455 of 1913.)
ĀṞAGAḶŪR, ATTUR TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Chōḷēśvara temple, north base.

This records a royal order addressed to Vilāḍarāyar and puravariyār
instructing them to note that he had chosen to give up his entitlement to poṉvari,
kāśāyam, kāttigai-ppachchai, nall-ērudu, naṟpaśu and such tax-incomes (āyam)
in respect of 3000 kuḻi of land in two plots, called maṉavan-ēndal and ulakaṅkātta-
perumāḷ-ēndal in Kīḻmagaṟūr granted to God Tāyilu-nalla-chōḷīśvaram-uḍaiya-
nāyaṉār of Āṟagaḷūr, by Kumaranadai Kayūr-ttēvaṉ. The land concerned was
made tirunāmattu-kkāṇi-iṟai-ili (tax-free land held directly by the God/temple).
The order was attested by the same two officials as in No. 300 above.

l.ta:
vilāṭarāyarum puravariyārum ceyyakkaṭavapaṭi
āṟakaḷur uṭaiyār tāyilu nalla coḷi

kiḻmakaḷūr maṉavaṉentallum ulakaṅkātta perumāḷentallum
kumārantaikayūrt tevaṉ innāyaṉāṟku eḻāvatu

inscription.head:
No. 302.
(A.R. No. 456 of 1913.)
TAḌĀVŪR, ATTUR TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Ēkāmranāthasvāmin temple, south wall.

This inscription records a royal order addressed to Vilāḍarāyar and
purvariyār for their execution, to the effect that the land called uḍai-kuḷam alias
vīra-māgadhaṉ-ēndal and the surrounding dry crop land (puñchey) west of the
channel (nīr-kōvai) from the big lake of Vīra-chōḻanallūr, along with (entitlement
to ?) the taxes (āyam), poṉvari, kāśāyam, kāttikai-ppachchai, mātru-chānti, ūśivāśi,
nall-ērudu, naṟ-ppaśu etc. as also āḷ-amaiñji-tēvai had been granted to God
Tiruvēkambamuḍaiya-nāyaṉār of Tiḍāvūr as dēvadāna-iṟaili (tax-free land
granted to God).

l.ta:
vilāṭar[ā]yarum puravariyārum ceyyakkaṭavapaṭi
tiṭāvūr uṭaiyār tiruvekampamu-

ṭaiya nāyaṉārkku vīracoḻa nallūril periyeri nīrkovaikku
meṟku uṭai-

kuḷamāṉa vīramākataṉ entalum ivvental cūḻnta puṉceyum

śrīmāhe

inscription.head:
No. 303.
(A.R. No. 457 of 1913.)
TAḌAVŪR, ATTUR TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Ekāmranāthasvāmin temple, south wall.

This inscription records a royal order declaring as dēvadāṉa-iṟai-ili (tax-free
land granted to the temple) the wet land (nan-chey) dry-crop land (pun-chey)
māvaḍai (game animals existing in the land or pen for village cattle) and
maravaḍai (all kinds of trees in the land) being enjoyed as iḷai-aiyā-podu (?)
by the temples of Tiruvēka[mbam-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār] and Kuṟṟam-eṟinda-perumāḷ,
for the purpose of providing food-offerings and carrying out repairs in the
temples.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [|*] vijayarācentira dēvaṟku 10āvatu mutal ••
vūr tiruveka ••• ••• •••• kum kuṉṟa

nancey punceyum māva-

ṭai maravaṭaiyum tiruvamutu tiruppaṇikku ivvāṇṭu mutal
tevatāṉa iṟaiyiliyāka

inscription.head:
No. 304.
(A.R. No. 458 of 1913.)
TAḌĀVŪR, ATTUR TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Ēkāmranāthasvāmin temple, south wall.

This inscription records that the temple of Tiruvēkambam-uḍaiya-nāyanār
of Mēl-Gaṅgappāḍi alias Tiḍāvār in Āṟṟūr-kūṟṟam in Milāḍu alias Jaṉaṉātha-
vaḷanāḍu was structured in stone (tirukkaṟṟaḷi-tiruppaṇi) with the help of gold
ornaments of the temple, part of it presented by donors earlier and the rest (20
kaḻañju in weight) presented (at 10 kaḻañju each) by Nāyaṉār (the king) and
his junior queen (iḷaiya-pīrāṭṭiyār). The responsibility for this work was
undertaken by one individual, who was Śrī-māhēśvara-kkaṇkāṇi-āṇḍār of the
temple, ūrār, dēvakanmi and accountant of the temple.

edition.l: sva[sti]śrī [|*] tiripuvanaccakkaravaṟtikaḷ maturaiyum pāṇṭi-

ya nāyanār koyil tirukkaṟṟaḷi tiruppaṇikku yiṉṉāyanār
tiruvāparaṇam mā[na]

paṭṭam muṉṉāḷil pirākkaṇapaṭṭam munnāḷil kulottuṅka

yil kaṇakkaṉum ikkoyillil tānattārum yipponniṭṭu i
tiruppa-

inscription.head:
No. 305.
(A.R. No. 459 of 1913.)
TAḌĀVŪR, ATTUR TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Ēkāmranāthasvāmin temple, north wall.

This inscription records a royal order addressed to the tānattār of the temple
of Tiruvēkambam-uḍaiyār of Tiḍāvūr intimating them the grant of the dry-crop
land after leaving out 7000 kuḻi of wet crop land in the bloc called Veṉṟa-perumāḷ
that was kāṇippaṟṟu, situated along the main channel (tala-kāl) outside the village,
which was lying waste overgrown with wood, as kuḍi-nīṅga-dēvadāṉa-iṟai-ili
(tax-free land without the involvement of the ejection of the erstwhile possessors).
The tānattār were asked to arrange for the bringing of the land under cultivation
with the help of the channel and dam in due proportion to the extent of the
land under the crop. With the help of this grant, they were to maintain the worship
of the God, carry out necessary repairs to the temple and also meet the expenses
on the day of the festival. The order was attested by Ādittaṉ Gaṇavati-āḻvāṉ
alias Vāṇādarāyan of Kappalūr alias Ulagaḷanda-chōḻanallūr in Muttūr-kūṟṟam
in Pāṇḍi-maṇḍalam.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [|*] aruḷicceyal tiṭāvūril uṭaiyār
tiruvekampamuṭaiyār koyilt tāṉattārkku i-

vvūr veḷiyil veṉṟa perumāḷeṉ talakāl vaḻi
kāṇippaṟṟāṉa nilam aṉaittāṇṭum pāḻāy kāṭeḻuṉtu
ki-

ṭakkayil patiṉeḻāvatu vaikāci mātam mutal iṉnāyaṉārk[ku*]

vārapaṟṟukku vaṭakkum mel ellai iṉnāyaṉār paḻa-

ṉ tevatāṉa iṟaiyilikku kiḻakkum vaṭavellai āṟṟukku
teṟkkum iṉṉāṉkkellaikkuṭpaṭṭa naṉ[c]e ku-

ḻi eḻāyiramum nīkki uḷḷa puṉceyum kuṭi niṅkā tevatāṉa
iṟaiyiliy[ā]ka tantom iṉnilam kaik-

vittuk koṇṭu iṉnāyaṉārkku pūcaikkum tiruppaṇikkum
tirunāḷaḻivukkum uṭalāka poṉvari uḷḷiṭṭa aṉait-

ātittaṉ kaṇavati āḻvāṉāṉa vāṇātarāya-

edition.l:
ṉeḻuttu— tulyam— After some space the following line is inscribed kālai eḻu itu kaṅkaṉāṭāḻvāṉ āṇai
āḷāka and two other letters the latter of which in a inscribed is detached space.

inscription.head:
No. 306.
(A.R. No. 460 of 1913.)
TAḌĀVŪR, ATTUR TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Ēkāmranāthasvāmin temple, north wall.

This records the perpetual grant of 500 kuḻi of wet land and some dry cops
land (2000 kuḻi ?) out of the Vārapaṟṟu in Taḍavūr with the right to sell and
mortgage, to Magadēśaṉ Ulagaṅ-kātta-Vāṇakōvaraiyaṉ Perudakāḷapparaiyaṉ
Pakkam-aḻagiyāṉ. The order was attested by Siṁhaḷar[āyaṉ].

edition.l: svastiśrī [|*] cimakatecaṉ ulakaṅkā-

l.ta: paṭṭa naṉcey kuḻi aññūṟum puṉavā vaḻi-
kku meṟkku eṟukāl puṉcey kuḻi iraṇ[ṭāyiramu*]-

l.note: ppaṭi has been written like monoṅram.

inscription.head:
No. 307.
(A.R. No. 461 of 1913.)
TAḌĀVŪR, ATTUR TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Ēkāmranāthasvāmin temple, north wall.
Portions of this inscription have been erased.

This inscription records the grant as mudal aḍaṅgā-iṟai-ili (tax free with
the mudal having been left out of account) inclusive of the levies kāttikai-
ppaichchai, mātṛi-śānti, āḷ-amañji etc. of (the village ?) Periyēri, already in the
vāra-paṟṟu (wherein the lands concerned contributed a fixed proportion of the
produce to the God or any person entitled to it) of God Tiruvēgambam-uḍaiya-
nāyinār of Taḍāvūr in Mēlgaṅgaipāḍi-nāḍu in Āṟṟūr-kūṟṟam in Milāḍu alias
Janaṉātha-vaḷanāḍu to the same god by Kulōttuṅga-chōḻa-Vāṇakōvaraiyaṉ. Besides
this donor, Edirili-chōḻa-vēḷān also certified the document. The purpose of the
gift was to meet the expenses on the worship of the God, the maintenance
of the building of the temple and on the conduct of festivals. cf. No. 308 below

edition.l:
svastiśrī [|*] kulottuṅkacoḻatevaṟkku yāṇṭu 5 vatu

inscription.head:
No. 308.
(A.R. No. 462 of 1913.)
inscription.p: TAḌĀVŪR, ATTUR TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
Ēkāmranāthasvāmin temple, north wall.

This records states that Kulōttuṅga-chōḻa Vāṇa-kōvaraiyan gifted one village
(name lost) in Īrājakēsari-nāḍu inclusive of the wet-land, drycrop land and
residential land in that village, as also the levies (āyam āḷ-amañji-tēvai) and all
āyams in respect of that village as dēvadāna-iṟai-ili (tax free land as gifted to
temple) for the purpose of the worship, of the repair and maintanence of the
premises of the temple and the expenses on the day of the festival for God
Tiruvēgaṁbam-uḍaiya-nāyanār of Tiḍāvūr. The record was attested by, besides
the donor, Edirili-chōḻa-vēḷāṉ. cf. No. 307 above.

inscription.head:
No. 309.
(A.R. No. 463 of 1913.)
inscription.p: TAḌĀVŪR, ATTUR TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
Ēkāmranāthasvāmin temple, north wall.
This inscription is written indifferently.

It records a royal order addressed to Vilāḍarāyar and puravariyār for
execution, granting all the land in the village Mēl-gaṅgappāḍi alias Puttūr in
Āṟṟūr-kkūṟṟam with inclusion of all āyam (levies) for the worship and
maintenance of the premises of the temple of Tiruvēkaṁbam-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār of
Taḍāvūr. It was attested by Tāyiluṉalla-perumāḷ and Viḻuppādarāyaṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 310.
(A.R. No. 464 of 1913.)
inscription.p: TAḌĀVŪR, ATTUR TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
Ēkāmranāthasvāmin temple, stone through the sacred tirtha.
This is in late characters.

It records that the through for the discarded decorations (ninmāṉiya) of the
God was made by vāṇiyan (merchant ?) Kāvaṉ Kūttāṇḍāṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 311.
(A.R. No. 465 of 1913.)
inscription.p: KOKKARĀYANPET, TIRUCHCHENGODU TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
Brahmapurīśvara temple, South wall.

p.b:
Achyutadēva-mahārāya Śaka 1453, Vikriti, Aṟpaśi 25,
śu. 12, Monday, Puṇarpūśam
Irregular
p.note:
of. A.R.Ep., 1914, p. 68. L.D.Swamikannu Pillai has stated that 1530 A.D. 24 October was Aṟpaśi
25 and it was Monday. Even according to his own Indian Ephemeris, Vol. V, Aṟpaśi 25 was
equivalent to october 25, which was a Tuesday in the year 1530 A.D. corresponded to Vikṛiti
but the Śaka year was 1453 current.

inscription.p:
This inscription records that Chāma-nayiṉār-atiyāriyaḷ (adhikārigaḷ), the
agent of Taṇa[Kishṇa]rāya-nāyakkar granted as mānyam, the village, Paṭṭilūr-kāla-
uḷḷi-Tagaḍappāḍi-pāḷaiyam alias Vaḍivuḍaiya-maṅgaipuram to God Biṟamīśvaram-
uḍaiya-tambirāṉār.

inscription.head:
No. 312.
(A.R. No. 466 of 1913.)
inscription.p: KOKKARĀYANPET, TIRUCHCHENGODU TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
Brahmapurīśvara temple, east wall, left of entrance.

p.b:
Kṛishṇadēva-mahārāya Śaka 1438, Īśvara, Tai 29,
chaturddaśi, Monday,
Pūśam, Āyush-yōgam = 1518
A.D., January 25 (The Śaka
Year was 1439. The tithi was
śu. 14).

inscription.p:
Some portions of the inscription have been erased. It records a royal grant
of a village (Tiruvāḻi-maṅgalam ?) which included all types of land nañchey,
puñchey, garden-land etc., to God Biramīśvaram-uḍaiya-tambirāṉār. While listing
the exceptions from the grant, Śaṅgamāpuram is mentioned. Its significance is
not clear.

inscription.head:
No. 313.
(A.R. No. 467 of 1913.)
inscription.p: KOKKARĀYANPET, TIRUCHCHENGODU TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
Brahmapurīśvara temple, east wall, left of entrance.
This inscription is damaged and unfinished. It is in late characters.

It records the grant of some hamlets for the decoration of the God
Biramīśvaram-uḍaiya-tambirāṉār in [Mā]taḷipēṭṭai as also the maintenance and
repairs of the temple of the said God and for paying the maintenance to a dance-
master naṭṭuvaṉ (appointed to serve in the temple ?) by Nāgarasaiyyaṉ, the agent
of Kiṭṭiṇa-nāyak[kar].

l.ta:

[mā]ṟivu peṭṭai atil piṟivāṉa piḻiyampāḷaiyamum
mayaṉāṇṭāṉ pāḷaiyamum nam-

inscription.head:
No. 314.
(A.R. No. 468 of 1913.)
inscription.p: KOKKARĀYANPET, TIRUCHCHENGODU TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
Brahmapurīśvara temple, Four slabs built into the floor.

The top (beginning) as well as the ends of all the four slabs containing this
inscription are damaged.

Contains portions of the meykkīrtti starting with Pugaḻmādu viḷaṅga etc
of the king (cf. SII, VII, No. 133). It records the arrangement (nimandham) made
for meeting the expenses to be incurred for conducting the midnight service
(tiruv-arddhayāma) for God Brahmīśvaram-uḍaiya-mādēvar in •••• maṅgalam
in Nāḍāḻvā-nāḍu in Koṅgu alias Vīrachōḻa-maṇḍalam by Gaṅgai-koṇḍān
Ammāiappan alias Mādhavarājan, for the sake of the restoration of the health
(tirumēni-kalyāṇam-āga) of [Chakkaravaṛ]tti Kulōttuṅga-chōḻa-dēvar. It appears
that some tax-income was set apart for this purpose. Some provision was also
made for the renovation of the temple. It is of interest, that the prices in gold
for various commodities are given in this record.

One detached piece in a different hand written in characters of about the
same period, of which the beginning is lost and which ends abruptly, records
the endowment of ten kaḻañju of gold by Kaḍamba-māri, the brahmin wife of
Kāppiyaṉ Vēdi for feeding ten brahmins on the days of each saṁkrānti, the gold
having been deposited with the mūla-pariḍai of Akaḻimaṅgalam in Kiḻār-kūṟṟam
in Nittavinōda-vaḷanāḍu. The latter body, sabhaiyār is mentioned in the
inscriptions at Chakkarappaḷḷi, locality of Ayyampēṭṭai, Papanasam Taluk,
Tanjavur District - B 308-309 of 1965-66).

edition.p: 1. In the same bundle one fragment written in the 11th century is found.

inscription.head:
No. 315.
(A.R. No. 469 of 1913.)
KŪGAḶŪR, GOBICHETTIPALAIYAM TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Madhyapurīśvara temple, north and east walls.

The writing of the inscription on the stone has been erased and damaged
and some stones containing portions of the inscription are also missing. However
as the text is a copy of an inscription found at Pāriyūr in the same taluk, and
district (No. 185 of 1910) it has been possible to reconstruct the text.

It records a royal order exempting several temples from the payment of
Voṭṭachchu as it had proved too burdensome. They were also freed from the
obligation to pay the demands of officials and others. Mention is made of 600
achchu and another 100 achchu deposited, the latter being the amount diverted
from dues payable to Toṇḍaimānār-patuvāra-achchu (?) payable on these said
amount of achchus which was also exempted from payment.

inscription.head:
No. 316.
(A.R. No. 470 of 1913.)
inscription.p: KŪGAḶŪR, GOBICHETTIPALAIYAM TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
Madhyapurīśvara temple, east wall.

The begining and end of the lines as also the continuation of the inscription
have been lost. Even in the available portion, the stone has flaked off resulting
in the loss of a few lines. However, the records is interesting in so far as it refers
to aṟudi-kkarai (i.e.) the final settlement in the process of rotation of blocs of
land among the holders of shares in the village.

The record says that Naṭṭūrāṇḍāṉ, a nāṭṭu-kkāmiṇḍar, who probably belonged
to Vaḍakarai-nāḍu endowed a lamp and deposited 10 paṇam with the ūr and
ūrāḷigaḷ of Kūvalūr in Kāñchikkūval-nāḍu. The latter, on accepting the endowed
amount, allotted against it some land (taṟai) as aṟudi-kkaṟai (final allotment
without the liablility to be changed at the next reallocation-final karai-of
the blocs of land in the village). The said land was given to the charge of a
śivabrāhmaṇa of Bhāradvāja-gōtram named Āvuḍaiyār Nāyaka-nambiyār alias
Periyanāṭṭu-bhaṭṭar.

There are nine other fragments of at least three other inscriptions. One of
them quotes the year 10 of a king (name lost), another a gift for a twilight
lamp (sandhyā-dīpam) and another a gift of 10 paṇam for a lamp.

l.note: Apparently this is a corruption of ciṟakka

vaṭakarai nāṭṭuk karuva[ḷi] ••• ••• ••••

of the lines have been lost.

l.ta:
ttu āvuṭaiyār
nāyaka nampiyārāna periyanāṭṭup paṭṭaṟku nāṭṭuk kāmiṇṭa
naṭṭurāṇṭānen cemmaṇkuḻiyiṟ paṭṭi aṟutikkāṇṭu
kuṭukkaiyil in nilaṅ kaikkoṇṭu uṭaiyār naṭṭu rāṇṭā
l.note:
Continuation lost. There are nine fragments of inscriptions in the same place which read as
follows (see next page).

inscription.head:
No. 317.
(A.R. No. 471 of 1913.)
inscription.p: KŪGAḶŪR, GOBICHETTIPALAIYAM TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
Madhyapurīśvara temple, above the entrance.

This inscription records the endowment of ten paṇam for a twilight lamp
for God Naṭṭūrāṇḍār by Chūkkaṉ Naṭṭūraṉ, an agriculturist (veḷḷāḷaṉ) of Kūvalūr
in Kāñchikkūval-nāḍu. The amount was deposited with Āvuḍaiyār Nāyaka-
nambiyār of Bhāradvāja-gōtra, a śivabrahmaṇa, having kāṇi of the temple.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [|*] naṉmaṅkalañ ciṟakka [|*] viravallāḷa dēvar
pridhivirājyam paṇṇiyaruḷā niṉṟa pramādauta sam

l.ta:
ttu taimmāta mutal k[āñ]cikkūval nāṭṭil kūvalūril
veḷḷāḷaṉ pūmakaḷil cokkaṉ

naṭṭuraṉen nammur nāyiṉār naṭṭurāṇṭārkkuc cantiyātipa
viḷakku oṉṟukku ikkoyiṟ kāṇiyuṭaiya

ttu āvuṭaiyār nāyaka nampiyār
vacam kuṭutta ippaṇam

inscription.head:
No. 318.
(A.R. No. 472 of 1913.)
inscription.p: KŪGAḶŪR, GOBICHETTIPALAIYAM TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
Madhyapurīśvara temple, above the entrance.

Major portion of the inscription is built in at the right end, obscuring the
purport of the record.

edition.l: svastiśrī [|*] naṉma

inscription.head:
No. 319-329.
(A.R. No. 473 to 483 of 1913.)
AMARĀVATI, SATTENAPALLE TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Inscribed slabs now preserved in the Government Museum, Madras.
Published in Epigraphia Indica, Vol. XV, p. 262, 266, 267, 271, 272, 273.

inscription.head:
No. 330-331.
(A.R. No. 504-505 of 1913.)
inscription.p: MADRAS.
Slabs in the underground room in the Government Museum, Madras

These are two lines written in southern characters of about the 6th century
A.D. They are in the Sanskrit language which is corrupt. Each line seems to be
a separate record as the second line starts with a spiral auspicious symbol. But
both the lines might also have been connected. The first line tells that the Vishṇu-
gṛihaam done by the Chaḷuka Ānandaputra of Bōṁrikāpura-vāstavya, might
endure till the moon and sun last. The second line refers to the work done by
Jaya-kīrtti-Śrī-kīrtti a stone-artisan, son of Āḍūr-āchāryya, a scholar in all śāstras
(branches of learning).

On a different slab is an inscription written in the same language and
characters, which refers to the same Ānandaputra of Bōrikāpura-vāstavya and
the erection of (the temple of ?) Kīrtti-nārāyaṇa.

l.de:
svastiśrī [|*] berilāpuravāstavyānanda putraṭhaḍhatha [caku]ka viṣṇugrahacca kattivyaṁ sāvatcandra
divākarau

āḍūrācāyyī putroyam jayakīrti śrīkirttita [śilpi] karmtatidaṁ kūtvā sarnvaśāstra
viśāradaḥ ||

inscription.head:
No. 332.
(A.R. No. 506 of 1913.)
inscription.p: MADRAS.
Slabs in the underground room in the Government Museum, Madras

Written in the Sanskrit language and in Southern characters of about the
6th century A.D., This inscription seems to record the obeisance to the chatiya
with the big idol, of Paṁchavāri-chaṁdra-kuśalakāri on his visit.

l.note:
Cf. Siviramamurti, Bulletin of the Madras Government Museum-Amaravati sculptures in the
Madras Government Museum, New series, General section, Vol. IV P. 285, No. 57. Probably
the same inscription had been read as follows:
(1) āsrutaḥ śrī-mahā-Vimbam
(2) vikāraḥ pa(gha) vāri paṇḍu
(3) kuśalakārih śrī-vyārā
(4) vaigaven da.

inscription.head:
No. 332.
(A.R. No. 510 of 1913.)
inscription.p: MADRAS.
On Slab in the ground outside the Government Museum building.

The stone containing this inscription is mutilated. Fragments of the meykīrtti
commencing with Tirumagaḷ pōla of the king are available at the beginning of
the inscription. It is also clear that it records an endowment of sheep for a
perpetual lamp. Kōvūr in Māṅgāḍu-nāḍu is mentioned.

inscription.head:
No. 334.
(A.R. No. 511 of 1913.)
inscription.p: Slabs in the ground outside the Government Museum building.

p.b:
Vīra-Kampaṇa-uḍaiyār [Kali] 447., Śaka 12[9*]3, Virodhikṛit,
Mithuna ba. 5, Wednesday, Aviṭṭam =
1371 A.D., June 4
p.note:
Cf. A.R.Ep., 1914, p. 67, L.D.S. Pillai has equated the details of date to 4th May. But he also
says that the record gives Dhanus as the month. While giving the Śaka year the writer of
the record had skipped toṇṇūṟṟu in line 7.

inscription.p: Same as 293 of 1895. Published in S.I.I, V. No. 857.

inscription.head:
No. 335.
(A.R. No. 512 of 1913.)
inscription.p: MADRAS.
On a stone set up at the entrance into the Government Museum.

Same as 290-91 of 1895. In line 12 of No. 854 of SII. Vol. V of the published text what has been read as pala civa
maṉṟāṭi can be better read as pallava maṉṟāṭiPublished in SII, V. Nos. 854-55.

inscription.head:
No. 336.
(A.R. No. 513 of 1913.)
inscription.p: MADRAS.
On a stone set up at the entrance into the Government Museum.

p.b: Tribhuvanachakravartti Vīrarājēndra (Kulōttuṅga III) Year 5, Ṛishabha śu. 3, Sunday, Punarpūśam.

inscription.p: Same as 297 of 1895. Published in S.I.I, V. Nos. 861.

inscription.head:
No. 337.
(A.R. No. 514 of 1913.)
inscription.p: MADRAS.
Slabs in the ground outside the Government Museum building.

p.note:
In the Annual report of Epigraphy. 1914 against the number, the Śaka date a given as 1452
which is correct and the reading 1450 in the published reference is not.
p.b:
Vikriti,
KarkaTaka śu. 13, Monday,
PUśam Irregular.

inscription.p: Same as 299 of 1895. Published in S.I.I, V. Nos. 863.

inscription.head:
No. 338.
(A.R. No. 515 of 1913.)
inscription.p: MADRAS.
On a stone set up at the entrance into the Government Museum building.

Same as 295 of 1895. Published in S.I.I, V. Nos. 859. In the published text lines 10-12 have not been properly read. The lines actually read:
10. ḻimāta mutal tirunīrmalai 11. nāyaṉār nir[va]ṇa 12 ṇapperu The record seems to continue
to line 21 on the phase, which contains the published text and also on another phase of the
stone. But the writing is very much damaṅed and nothing coṅent can be made out.

inscription.head:
No. 339.
(A.R. No. 516 of 1913.)
inscription.p: MADRAS.
On a stone set up at the entrance into the Government Museum.

p.note:
Though in the published text, the Śaka date has been read as 1530, it is possible to read it
as 1531, which corresponded to the cyclic year Saumya quoted.
p.b:
Saumya, Ṛishabha,
Ēkādaśi, Friday, Rēvati = 1609 A.D.,
May 19. the tithi was ba. 11.

inscription.p:
Same as 294 of 1895. The text as published can be improved as follows:
Line 12 lat tāriyatapār tevama
ḷine 13 ṇyala kiṟā[ma]māṉa poḻica
ḷine 16 ṉaviyappar akuroyicupara u-
ḷine 25 kku vaṭakku tevikolla
ḷine 26 yum cuṟṟupura[t]tu meṭum ku-Published in SII, V. Nos. 858.

inscription.head:
No. 340.
(A.R. No. 526 of 1913.)
inscription.p: MADRAS.

On the base of a Jaina image in the Archaeological Showroom in the
Government Museum.

This inscription opens with a salutation to Śāntinātha and contains a single
verse in Śardūlavikrīḍitam in the Sanskrit language recording the installation and
consecration of the image of Śānti-Jina by a cheif referred to as Sāḷvēndra and
Sāḷva-nṛipa.

l.de:
śrīśāntināthāya namaḥ | sā ḷaveṁdraḥ sakalāvanīśa tilakaḥ samyakta cūḍāmaṇiḥ sāhityāṁ
buḷī caṁdramāḥ sucaritā tasyākṣavakṣalo | taññaḷarābdhi saṁkṣavanidhuḥ kṣīsāḷana devonūpakṣsoyaṁ
śāntijjinaṁ nidhyāya nidhina sumyak pratikṣaṁ vyadhān

inscription.p: VINUKOṆḌA. VINUKONDA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
On a ruined temple of Narasiṁhasvāmin at the foot of the hill.

p.b:
Gaṁna-nṛipati Śaka 1399 (nidhi-randhra-vahni
-śaśī) Hēmalamba, Kārttika
śu. 10, Thursday = 1477 A.D.,
October 16.

inscription.p:
This inscription contains a strings of verses in Sanskrit language and Telugu
script. After giving the pedigree of one Gaṁna-nṛipati, the Gadā-kshmāpati, who
belonged to the fourth varṇa (caste) and whose family is stated to have consisted
of warriors, records the construction of the temple of Lakshmī-Nṛisiṁha with
a lofty maṇṭapa and other appurtenances, at Vinikoṇḍa, in the middle territory
to the north-east of Śrīśaila.

l.note: The anusvara is written in the next line.
A Fortice with the head to the proper right at the end of the inscription.
